passage_title: Can the planet's coral reefs be saved?

sentences.sentence: Conservationists have put the final touches to a giant artificial reef they have been assembling at the world-renowned Zoological Society of London (London Zoo).
sentences.translation: 环保人士已经为一个巨大的人工珊瑚礁完成最后润色；他们一直在举世闻名的伦敦动物学会（伦敦动物园）组装这个珊瑚礁。

structure.text: have put the final touches to a giant artificial reef
at the world-renowned Zoological Society of London (London Zoo)

difficult_points: put the final touches to 表示完成最后阶段的完善工作。
they have been assembling 是省略关系词的定语从句，修饰 a giant artificial reef。
have been assembling 使用现在完成进行时，强调组装工作持续了一段时间。

sentences.sentence: Samples of the planet's most spectacular corals – vivid green branching coral, yellow scroll, blue ridge and many more species – have been added to the giant tank along with fish that thrive in their presence: blue tang, clownfish and many others.
sentences.translation: 地球上一些最壮观的珊瑚样本——鲜绿色枝状珊瑚、黄色卷叶珊瑚、蓝脊珊瑚以及更多物种——已经与在珊瑚环境中繁盛生长的鱼类一起被加入这个巨大水箱，其中包括蓝吊鱼、小丑鱼等。

structure.text: Samples of the planet's most spectacular corals
vivid green branching coral, yellow scroll, blue ridge and many more species
have been added to the giant tank
along with fish that thrive in their presence

difficult_points: 破折号中的内容列举 spectacular corals 的具体样本。
have been added 是现在完成被动语态，说明这些样本已被放入水箱。
that thrive in their presence 修饰 fish，其中 their 指珊瑚。

sentences.sentence: The reef is in the zoo’s new gallery, Tiny Giants, which is dedicated to the minuscule invertebrate creatures that sustain life across the planet.
sentences.translation: 这个珊瑚礁位于动物园的新展馆“小巨人”中，该展馆专门展示维系全球生命的微小无脊椎动物。

structure.text: is in the zoo’s new gallery, Tiny Giants
which is dedicated to the minuscule invertebrate creatures
that sustain life across the planet

difficult_points: Tiny Giants 是 the zoo’s new gallery 的同位语。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明这个展馆的主题。
that sustain life across the planet 修饰 creatures，强调微小生物的重要性。

sentences.sentence: The coral reef tank and its seven-metre-wide window form the core of the exhibition.
sentences.translation: 珊瑚礁水箱及其七米宽的观景窗构成了展览的核心。

structure.text: The coral reef tank and its seven-metre-wide window

difficult_points: 主语由 tank 和 window 两部分并列构成。
form the core of 表示构成展览最核心的部分。

sentences.sentence: 'Coral reefs are the most diverse ecosystems on Earth and we want to show people how wonderful they are,' said Paul Pearce-Kelly, senior curator of invertebrates and fish at the Zoological Society of London.
sentences.translation: 伦敦动物学会无脊椎动物和鱼类高级馆长 Paul Pearce-Kelly 说：“珊瑚礁是地球上最多样化的生态系统，我们想向人们展示它们多么奇妙。”

structure.text: Coral reefs are the most diverse ecosystems on Earth
and we want to show people how wonderful they are
senior curator of invertebrates and fish at the Zoological Society of London

difficult_points: 引号中包含两个并列分句，分别说明珊瑚礁的地位和展示目的。
how wonderful they are 是 show 的宾语从句。
senior curator... 是 Paul Pearce-Kelly 的同位语，说明其身份。

sentences.sentence: 'However, we also want to highlight the research and conservation efforts that are now being carried out to try to save them from the threat of global warming.'
sentences.translation: “不过，我们也想突出现在为拯救它们免受全球变暖威胁而正在进行的研究和保护工作。”

structure.text: we also want to highlight the research and conservation efforts
that are now being carried out
to try to save them from the threat of global warming

difficult_points: However 表示展览目的不只是展示美，也要展示保护。
that are now being carried out 修饰 research and conservation efforts，且为现在进行被动。
to try to save them... 表示开展这些工作的目的，them 指 coral reefs。

sentences.sentence: They want people to see what is being done to try to save these wonders.
sentences.translation: 他们希望人们看到，为拯救这些奇观，人们正在做些什么。

structure.text: to try to save these wonders

difficult_points: They 指伦敦动物园/保护人员。
want people to see 是“希望某人做某事”的结构。
what is being done 是宾语从句，使用现在进行被动表示正在开展的行动。

sentences.sentence: Corals are composed of tiny animals, known as polyps, with tentacles for capturing small marine creatures in the sea water.
sentences.translation: 珊瑚由被称为珊瑚虫的微小动物组成，这些动物有触手，用来捕捉海水中的小型海洋生物。

structure.text: with tentacles for capturing small marine creatures in the sea water

difficult_points: are composed of 表示珊瑚由微小动物构成。
known as polyps 是过去分词短语，说明 tiny animals 的名称。
for capturing... 表示触手的功能。

sentences.sentence: These polyps are transparent but get their brilliant tones of pink, orange, blue, green, etc. from algae that live within them, which in turn get protection, while their photosynthesising of the sun’s rays provides nutrients for the polyps.
sentences.translation: 这些珊瑚虫本身是透明的，但它们从体内共生的藻类那里获得粉色、橙色、蓝色、绿色等鲜艳色调；这些藻类反过来得到保护，同时它们对阳光的光合作用为珊瑚虫提供营养。

structure.text: but get their brilliant tones of pink, orange, blue, green, etc. from algae
while their photosynthesising of the sun’s rays provides nutrients for the polyps

difficult_points: but 连接透明本体与获得鲜艳颜色的对比。
that live within them 修饰 algae，them 指 polyps。
which in turn get protection 中 which 指 algae，说明藻类也从共生关系中受益。
while 从句说明藻类光合作用为珊瑚虫提供营养。

sentences.sentence: This comfortable symbiotic relationship has led to the growth of coral reefs that cover 0.1% of the planet's ocean bed while providing homes for more than 25% of marine species, including fish, molluscs, sponges and shellfish.
sentences.translation: 这种舒适的共生关系促成了珊瑚礁的生长；珊瑚礁覆盖了地球海床的0.1%，却为超过25%的海洋物种提供栖息地，包括鱼类、软体动物、海绵和贝类。

structure.text: has led to the growth of coral reefs
that cover 0.1% of the planet's ocean bed
while providing homes for more than 25% of marine species
including fish, molluscs, sponges and shellfish

difficult_points: This comfortable symbiotic relationship 指珊瑚虫与藻类互利共生。
has led to 表示这种关系造成了珊瑚礁生长的结果。
while providing... 强调珊瑚礁覆盖面积小，却支撑大量物种。

sentences.sentence: As a result, coral reefs are often described as the 'rainforests of the sea', though the comparison is dismissed by some naturalists, including David Attenborough.
sentences.translation: 因此，珊瑚礁常被描述为“海洋中的雨林”，不过包括 David Attenborough 在内的一些博物学家并不认同这种比较。

structure.text: coral reefs are often described as the 'rainforests of the sea'
though the comparison is dismissed by some naturalists

difficult_points: As a result 承接上一段珊瑚礁物种丰富的事实。
are described as 是被动结构，表示常见称呼。
though 引导让步从句，指出这种比喻并非所有人都接受。

sentences.sentence: 'People say you cannot beat the rainforest,' Attenborough has stated.
sentences.translation: Attenborough 曾说：“人们说没有什么比得上雨林。”
difficult_points: you cannot beat... 是 say 的宾语从句，表示“没有什么比得上”。
has stated 使用现在完成时，表示其观点曾被公开表达。
People say 表示一种普遍看法，而非作者自己的判断。

sentences.sentence: 'But that is simply not true.
sentences.translation: “但那根本不是真的。
difficult_points: But 表示 Attenborough 反驳前一句的普遍看法。
that 指“雨林无可超越”这一说法。
simply 强化否定语气，表示“完全、根本”。

sentences.sentence: You go there and the first thing you think is: where ... are the birds?
sentences.translation: 你到了那里，首先想到的是：鸟……在哪里？

structure.text: and the first thing you think is

difficult_points: there 指雨林。
the first thing you think 中省略关系词 that，修饰 thing。
where ... are the birds? 是直接疑问内容，省略号表现说话停顿。

sentences.sentence: Where are the animals?
sentences.translation: 动物在哪里？
difficult_points: 这是直接问句，延续前一句对雨林中可见动物的质疑。
the animals 泛指人们期待在雨林中看到的动物。

sentences.sentence: They are hiding in the trees, of course.
sentences.translation: 当然，它们都藏在树上。
difficult_points: They 指前一句的 animals。
are hiding 使用进行时，说明动物并非不存在，而是不容易被看见。
of course 表示解释很显然。

sentences.sentence: No, if you want beauty and wildlife, you want a coral reef.
sentences.translation: 不，如果你想要美景和野生生物，你想看的应该是珊瑚礁。

structure.text: if you want beauty and wildlife

difficult_points: No 用来否定雨林是最佳选择的观点。
if 从句提出条件：想看美景和野生生物。
you want a coral reef 表示珊瑚礁才是更合适的选择。

sentences.sentence: Put on a mask and stick your head under the water.
sentences.translation: 戴上面罩，把头伸到水下。

structure.text: and stick your head under the water

difficult_points: 这是祈使句，省略主语 you。
Put on 和 stick 是并列动作。
under the water 表示观察珊瑚礁的地点。

sentences.sentence: The sight is mind-blowing.'
sentences.translation: 那景象令人震撼。”
difficult_points: The sight 指水下珊瑚礁和海洋生物的景象。
mind-blowing 表示强烈震撼，语气非常肯定。

sentences.sentence: Unfortunately, these majestic sights are now under very serious threat, with the most immediate problem coming in the form of thermal stress.
sentences.translation: 不幸的是，这些壮丽景象如今正面临非常严重的威胁，其中最紧迫的问题表现为热应激。

structure.text: are now under very serious threat
with the most immediate problem coming in the form of thermal stress

difficult_points: Unfortunately 标示作者对珊瑚礁处境的负面评价。
under very serious threat 表示处于严重威胁之下。
with 后接独立结构，补充说明最直接的问题是什么。

sentences.sentence: Rising ocean temperatures are triggering bleaching events that strip reefs of their colour and eventually kill them.
sentences.translation: 不断上升的海洋温度正在引发白化事件，这些事件会使珊瑚礁失去颜色，并最终杀死它们。

structure.text: that strip reefs of their colour and eventually kill them

difficult_points: are triggering 使用现在进行时，强调白化事件正在被引发。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 bleaching events。
strip reefs of their colour 表示剥夺珊瑚礁颜色，即白化。

sentences.sentence: And that is just the start.
sentences.translation: 而这只是开始。
difficult_points: that 指上一句的海温升高和白化问题。
just the start 表示后面还有更多威胁。

sentences.sentence: Other menaces include ocean acidification, sea level increase, pollution by humans, deoxygenation and ocean current changes, while the climate crisis is also increasing habitat destruction.
sentences.translation: 其他威胁包括海洋酸化、海平面上升、人类污染、脱氧和洋流变化，同时气候危机也在加剧栖息地破坏。

structure.text: include ocean acidification, sea level increase, pollution by humans, deoxygenation and ocean current changes
while the climate crisis is also increasing habitat destruction

difficult_points: include 后列举多种威胁，构成并列宾语。
while 引导并列/对比状语，补充气候危机造成的另一影响。
is also increasing 使用进行时，强调栖息地破坏仍在加剧。

sentences.sentence: As a result, vast areas – including massive chunks of Australia’s Great Barrier Reef – have already been destroyed, and scientists advise that more than 90% of reefs could be lost by 2050 unless urgent action is taken to tackle global heating and greenhouse gas emissions.
sentences.translation: 结果，包括澳大利亚大堡礁大片区域在内的广阔海域已经遭到破坏；科学家警告说，除非采取紧急行动应对全球升温和温室气体排放，否则到2050年超过90%的珊瑚礁可能会消失。

structure.text: including massive chunks of Australia’s Great Barrier Reef
and scientists advise that more than 90% of reefs could be lost by 2050 unless urgent action is taken to tackle global heating and greenhouse gas emissions

difficult_points: including... 是插入性补充，说明 vast areas 包括大堡礁大片区域。
have already been destroyed 是现在完成被动，强调破坏已经发生。
unless 引导条件从句，说明避免90%以上珊瑚礁消失的前提。

sentences.sentence: Pearce-Kelly says that coral reefs have to survive really harsh conditions – wave erosion and other factors.
sentences.translation: Pearce-Kelly 说，珊瑚礁必须在非常严酷的条件下生存，包括海浪侵蚀和其他因素。

structure.text: that coral reefs have to survive really harsh conditions

difficult_points: that 引导宾语从句，说明 Pearce-Kelly 的观点。
have to survive 强调珊瑚礁本来就面对严酷环境。
破折号后的 wave erosion and other factors 解释 harsh conditions。

sentences.sentence: And 'when things start to go wrong in the oceans, then corals will be the first to react.
sentences.translation: 而且，“当海洋中开始出现问题时，珊瑚将是最先作出反应的。

structure.text: when things start to go wrong in the oceans
then corals will be the first to react

difficult_points: when 引导时间状语从句，说明珊瑚反应发生的条件。
go wrong 表示海洋系统出现问题。
the first to react 表示珊瑚对海洋变化极其敏感。

sentences.sentence: And that is exactly what we are seeing now.
sentences.translation: 而这正是我们现在看到的情况。
difficult_points: that 指前一句“珊瑚最先对海洋问题作出反应”的现象。
what we are seeing now 是表语从句。
exactly 强调当前观察到的情况完全符合这一判断。

expressions.meaning_zh: 我们现在所看到的情况

sentences.sentence: Coral reefs are dying and they are telling us that all is not well with our planet.'
sentences.translation: 珊瑚礁正在死亡，它们在告诉我们，我们的星球并不安好。”

structure.text: that all is not well with our planet

difficult_points: are dying 使用进行时，强调死亡正在发生。
they are telling us 是拟人化表达，表示珊瑚礁的状态传递警讯。
all is not well with our planet 表示地球整体状况出了问题。

expressions.meaning_zh: 情况并不正常/并不安好

sentences.sentence: However, scientists are trying to pinpoint hardy types of coral that could survive our overheated oceans, and some of this research will be carried out at London Zoo.
sentences.translation: 然而，科学家们正试图找出能够在过热海洋中存活的耐受型珊瑚，其中一些研究将在伦敦动物园进行。

structure.text: scientists are trying to pinpoint hardy types of coral
that could survive our overheated oceans
and some of this research will be carried out at London Zoo

difficult_points: However 表示从威胁转向科学应对。
that could survive... 修饰 hardy types of coral。
will be carried out 是将来被动语态，说明部分研究的地点。

sentences.sentence: 'Behind our ... coral reef tank we have built laboratories where scientists will be studying coral species,' said Pearce-Kelly.
sentences.translation: Pearce-Kelly 说：“在我们的……珊瑚礁水箱后面，我们建了实验室，科学家将在那里研究珊瑚物种。”

structure.text: where scientists will be studying coral species

difficult_points: Behind our ... coral reef tank 是地点状语，省略号表示引语中的停顿。
where 引导定语从句，修饰 laboratories。
will be studying 使用将来进行时，说明科学家将在这些实验室持续研究。

sentences.sentence: One aim will be to carry out research on species to find those that can survive best in warm, acidic waters.
sentences.translation: 其中一个目标将是对珊瑚物种开展研究，以找出最能在温暖、酸性水域中存活的种类。

structure.text: will be to carry out research on species
that can survive best in warm, acidic waters

difficult_points: One aim will be to... 表示研究目标之一。
to find those 是目的不定式，说明开展研究的目的。
that can survive best... 修饰 those，指最能适应特定水域的珊瑚。

sentences.sentence: Another will be to try to increase coral breeding rates.
sentences.translation: 另一个目标将是设法提高珊瑚繁殖率。

structure.text: will be to try to increase coral breeding rates

difficult_points: Another 省略 aim，指另一个研究目标。
to try to increase 表示尝试提升，而非已经实现。
coral breeding rates 指珊瑚繁殖发生的频率或速度。

sentences.sentence: 'Coral spawn just once a year,' he added.
sentences.translation: 他补充说：“珊瑚每年只产卵一次。”

structure.text: Coral spawn just once a year

difficult_points: Coral 在这里泛指珊瑚，spawn 是动词。
just once a year 强调自然繁殖频率很低。
he added 表示该信息是 Pearce-Kelly 继续补充的内容。

sentences.sentence: 'However, aquarium-based research has enabled some corals to spawn artificially, which can assist coral reef restoration efforts.
sentences.translation: “然而，以水族馆为基础的研究已经使一些珊瑚能够人工产卵，这可以帮助珊瑚礁恢复工作。

structure.text: aquarium-based research has enabled some corals to spawn artificially
which can assist coral reef restoration efforts

difficult_points: However 表示与自然状态下一年产卵一次形成转折。
has enabled...to... 表示研究已经让某些珊瑚能够人工产卵。
which 指人工产卵这一成果，说明其对恢复工作的帮助。

expressions.meaning_zh: 以水族馆为基础的研究

sentences.sentence: And if this can be extended for all species, we could consider the launching of coral-spawning programmes several times a year.
sentences.translation: 如果这种方法能够扩展到所有物种，我们就可以考虑每年多次启动珊瑚产卵项目。

structure.text: if this can be extended for all species
we could consider the launching of coral-spawning programmes several times a year

difficult_points: this 指人工促使珊瑚产卵的方法。
can be extended 是被动结构，表示该方法能否推广。
could consider 表示在条件满足后可以考虑，而非已经决定。

sentences.sentence: That would be a big help in restoring blighted reefs.'
sentences.translation: 那将对恢复受损珊瑚礁大有帮助。”
difficult_points: That 指一年多次启动珊瑚产卵项目的可能做法。
would be 表示假设条件下的结果。
in restoring blighted reefs 说明帮助的具体方向。

sentences.sentence: Research in these fields is being conducted in laboratories around the world, with the London Zoo centre linked to this global network.
sentences.translation: 这些领域的研究正在世界各地的实验室中进行，伦敦动物园中心也与这一全球网络相连。

structure.text: is being conducted in laboratories around the world
with the London Zoo centre linked to this global network

difficult_points: is being conducted 是现在进行被动，说明研究正在开展。
these fields 指寻找耐受珊瑚和提高繁殖率等研究领域。
with 后接独立结构，说明伦敦动物园中心与全球网络的关系。

sentences.sentence: Studies carried out in one centre can then be tested in others.
sentences.translation: 在一个中心开展的研究随后可以在其他中心接受测试。

structure.text: Studies carried out in one centre

difficult_points: carried out in one centre 是过去分词短语，修饰 Studies。
can then be tested 是情态动词加被动结构。
others 指其他研究中心或实验室。

sentences.sentence: The resulting young coral can then be displayed in the tank in Tiny Giants.
sentences.translation: 由此产生的幼珊瑚随后可以在“小巨人”展馆的水箱中展示。

structure.text: in the tank in Tiny Giants

difficult_points: resulting 表示这些幼珊瑚是前述研究或培育的结果。
can then be displayed 是情态动词加被动结构。
in the tank in Tiny Giants 指展示地点。

sentences.sentence: 'The crucial point is that the progress we make in making coral better able to survive in a warming world can be shown to the public and encourage them to believe that we can do something to save the planet's reefs,' said Pearce-Kelly.
sentences.translation: Pearce-Kelly 说：“关键在于，我们在让珊瑚更能适应变暖世界方面取得的进展，可以展示给公众，并鼓励他们相信我们能够采取行动拯救地球上的珊瑚礁。”

structure.text: that the progress we make in making coral better able to survive in a warming world can be shown to the public and encourage them to believe that we can do something to save the planet's reefs
we make in making coral better able to survive in a warming world
that we can do something to save the planet's reefs

difficult_points: that 引导表语从句，解释 The crucial point 的具体内容。
we make... 是省略关系词的定语从句，修饰 progress。
can be shown 和 encourage 是并列谓语，说明研究进展既可展示也可激励公众。
that we can do something... 是 believe 的宾语从句。

sentences.sentence: 'Saving our coral reefs is now a critically important ecological goal.'
sentences.translation: “拯救我们的珊瑚礁如今是一项至关重要的生态目标。”
difficult_points: Saving our coral reefs 是动名词短语作主语。
now 强调拯救珊瑚礁在当前形势下的紧迫性。
critically important 强化 ecological goal 的重要程度。
